item.title: LG Energy Eyes 30% Energy Storage Sales Mix To Counter EV Slump
item.description: LG Energy Solution Ltd. plans to boost its energy storage system business, aiming to increase the division’s revenue contribution to 30% by year-end amid a global electric vehicle slowdown.

item.title: SoftBank’s $40 Billion Loan for OpenAI Stake Draws More Banks
item.description: Banks that recently signed a $40 billion bridge loan with SoftBank Group Corp. for its investment in US tech giant OpenAI have attracted more lenders to the deal in syndication, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Indian Stocks’ April Rally Masks Fragile Underpinnings
item.description: Higher oil prices, persistent foreign outflows pose risks to the market.

item.title: Copper Snaps Five-Day Decline on China Manufacturing Expansion
item.description: Copper climbed in line with most industrial metals after Chinese factory activity expanded despite disruptions to supply chains and rising input costs caused by the Iran war.

item.title: Lenders HSBC, NAB Earnings Outlook Dampened by Middle East War Risks
item.description: Asia Pacific’s main lenders will offer diverging paths navigating the fallout of the Iran war, with HSBC Holdings Plc and National Australia Bank Ltd. more exposed than Singaporean peers.

item.title: Australia Regulator Threatens Enforcement for Poor AI Controls
item.description: Australia’s top prudential regulator said it will take action against companies that fail to adequately control cybersecurity threats, as concerns within the industry mount over Anthropic PBC’s latest AI model Mythos.

item.title: New Zealand Firms Turn Pessimistic as Costs Rise, Demand Wobbles
item.description: New Zealand business sentiment turned negative for the first time since 2023 as firms face rising costs and shrinking demand in response to the Middle East conflict, a new survey showed.

item.title: China’s Factory Activity Expands Despite Pressures From War
item.description: China’s factory activity expanded in April even as disruptions to supply chains and rising input costs caused by the Iran war reverberate around the world.

item.title: White House Opposes Anthropic Plan for Mythos Access, WSJ Says
item.description: The White House opposes Anthropic PBC’s plan to expand access to its Mythos AI model, the Wall Street Journal reported, a technology that the company says is powerful enough to enable dangerous cyberattacks.

item.title: India’s Rupee Defense Faces Tough Test as Capital Flows Stall
item.description: India’s efforts to steady the beleaguered rupee are likely to get harder in the coming months, as insufficient capital inflows replace speculative bets as the main pressure point.

item.title: Novo-Backed Drugmaker Avalyn Raises $300 Million in Upsized IPO
item.description: Avalyn Pharma Inc., a biopharma company developing inhaled treatments for rare lung diseases, raised $300 million in a US initial public offering that priced at the top of its marketed range.

item.title: US Urged to Turn ‘Rhetoric Into Reality’ on Pacific Fuel Supply
item.description: The US should back up its Pacific rhetoric with action by deploying fuel tankers to the region, a senior New Zealand minister said, as concerns grow about energy security in remote island nations vulnerable to supply disruptions.

item.title: Mirae Asset Sees Korea Rate Hikes on Chipmaker Wealth Boost
item.description: The extraordinary wealth generated from South Korean chipmakers will drive spending and inflation, spurring the nation’s central bank to raise interest rates at least three times over the coming year, according to Mirae Asset Global Investments.

item.title: OpenAI Meets Key AI Computing Capacity Goal Ahead of Schedule
item.description: OpenAI has met a key milestone for securing AI capacity in the US several years ahead of schedule, boosting the startup’s ambitious plans for data center expansion.

item.title: Startup Bringing Brains to AI Aims for $2.5 Billion Valuation
item.description: Thomas Reardon, who led the work on Meta Platforms Inc.’s Neural Band, is raising funds for a new startup called Flourish to develop energy-efficient artificial intelligence, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Alphabet, Amazon Outpace Meta in AI During Earnings Bonanza
item.description: A frenzied day of earnings reports offered a glimpse at how some of the world’s biggest tech companies are doing in artificial intelligence. The upshot: Alphabet Inc.’s Google is seeing a clear payoff from its AI spending, while Meta Platforms Inc. is lagging behind.

item.title: Oil Price Surge Threatens to Deepen Selloff of EM Asia’s Bonds
item.description: Higher-for-longer oil prices threaten a faster rise in emerging Asian bond yields, if history is any guide — a risk market watchers say isn’t yet fully priced in.

item.title: Fujitsu Falls Most in 11 Years After Forecast Misses Estimates
item.description: Fujitsu Ltd. shares fell as much as 14%, the most in 11 years, after the company’s fourth-quarter operating profit and full-year forecast missed analyst estimates.

item.title: Japan’s Factory Output Falls as Iran War Clouds Demand Outlook
item.description: Japan’s factory output fell as the war in Iran clouded prospects for global demand while also threatening manufacturers’ margins by way of higher energy costs.

item.title: Australia’s Top Migrant Group Hails From India, Trumping England
item.description: Indians are now Australia’s largest migrant group, supplanting the English for the first time ever, in a change that highlights the rise of immigration as an increasingly contentious political issue.

item.title: Samsung’s Chip Profit Soars 48-Fold Due to AI Spending Frenzy
item.description: Samsung Electronics Co.’s semiconductor arm beat expectations with a 48-fold jump in profit after AI’s reliance on memory delivered hefty margins.

item.title: SoftBank to Create and List AI Firm Roze in the US, FT Says
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp. plans to establish and float a standalone AI robotics and data center company called Roze in the US, the Financial Times reported, citing unidentified people familiar with the matter.

item.title: ASX Says Darren Yip to Become Interim CEO as Search Goes On
item.description: ASX Ltd. named Darren Yip, who oversees the exchange operator’s markets and listings divisions, as its interim boss amid a broader strategic review at the firm to lift risk management and governance practices.

item.title: Hong Kong’s Local Debt Market Becomes Surprise Funding Hotspot
item.description: Hong Kong’s once-quiet local currency bond market is quickly becoming one of Asia’s hottest funding venues, as corporate issuers seek stability in a city looking to lure more debt offerings.

item.title: RBNZ to Disclose Votes of Monetary Policy Committee Members
item.description: New Zealand’s central bank will disclose the votes of members of its Monetary Policy Committee if it is unable to reach a consensus decision on interest rates under the terms of a new charter agreed with the government.

item.title: Top US Apartment Owners AvalonBay, EQR to Discuss Combining
item.description: AvalonBay Communities Inc. and Equity Residential are considering a potential combination that could reshape the US apartment market, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Japan’s Two-Year Bond Sale Tests Demand After BOJ’s Hawkish Hold
item.description: Japan’s two-year bond auction on Thursday will test investor demand after recent policy signals from the Bank of Japan and the Federal Reserve added upward pressure to short-term rates.

item.title: Warsh Set to Inherit a Fed That Looks Ready to Fight Rate Cuts
item.description: As Kevin Warsh moves closer to becoming chair of the Federal Reserve, his future colleagues are moving further from supporting the resumption of interest rate cuts that President Donald Trump is expecting.

item.title: BOE Set to Hold Off Immediate Action on Rates: Decision Guide
item.description: The Bank of England is likely to keep interest rates on hold on Thursday as policymakers play for time amid the febrile standoff between the US and Iran.

item.title: AI-Led Rally in Asia Stocks Masks Deeper Damage From Iran War
item.description: Asia’s artificial intelligence-fueled rally is masking signs of strain, with gains in tech names overshadowing the impact of the US-Iran war on the broader market.
